events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the inspection outcome directly to `/app/answer.json`. Missing jack studs in inspected structural framing is a framing support defect, so I’m treating it as a failed/non-compliant condition with corrective rework required.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && cat > /tmp/answer.json.tmp <<'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"high\",
  \"work_stage\": \"tested/inspected\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 90,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.88,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Required jack studs are missing at framed opening support locations.\",
    \"Structural load path for headers or supported framing is incomplete and does not match typical IRC R502/R602 framing requirements or approved documents.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Reject the inspected framing area until corrected.\",
    \"Install the required jack studs in accordance with the approved drawings, header schedule, and applicable IRC R502/R602 framing provisions.\",
    \"Have the corrected framing re-inspected before concealment or follow-on finish work.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"Missing jack studs leave header or opening loads without the required bearing/load path. Because the framing has reached the inspection step and the defect affects structural support, the work is installed defective and non-compliant until corrected and re-inspected.\",
  \"references\": [\"IRC R502\", \"IRC R602\", \"approved framing drawings or inspection checklist\"]
}
EOF
mv /tmp/answer.json.tmp /app/answer.json
python3 -m json.tool /app/answer.json >/dev/null
ls -l /app/answer.json"
→ -rw-r--r-- 1 agent agent 1253 Jul  6 22:28 /app/answer.json
